entries.title: Australian GP
entries.headline: Russell Opens 2026 Season With Commanding Melbourne Victory
entries.summary: George Russell delivered a masterclass at Albert Park to claim victory at the 2026 Australian Grand Prix, with Mercedes sending an early statement to the rest of the field.
entries.body:
George Russell led from lights-to-flag in Melbourne to give Mercedes a dominant season opener, controlling the pace throughout and never looking troubled on his way to the chequered flag. A clean start off the front row put him immediately in clear air, and from there the Briton simply managed the gap with the composure that has defined his rise to the top of the sport. Behind him, the battle for the remaining podium positions was fiercely contested, with Lewis Hamilton bringing his Ferrari home in second after a late-race charge pushed him past a fading rival in the closing laps.
The midfield told a more chaotic story, with the new-for-2026 technical regulations producing close and unpredictable racing throughout the pack. Tyre degradation played a bigger role than many teams had anticipated at pre-season testing, catching several strategies out and shuffling the order in the final stint. Lando Norris salvaged a solid points finish for McLaren after a difficult qualifying, while both Audi entries showed genuine race pace that will have rival engineers taking notes. As the paddock heads to Shanghai, Russell leads the drivers' championship and Mercedes sit atop the constructors' standings — exactly the kind of start the Silver Arrows needed after a subdued 2025.
